subject: Sociology
title: Glocalization
description: A short lesson to help you apply Glocalization.

hook.text: McDonald's in India serves McAloo Tikki. In Japan, there's Teriyaki Burger. Global brands go local.

buildup.text: Glocalization describes how global products and ideas are adapted to fit local cultures and tastes.

discovery.text: Roland Robertson coined the term. Globalization doesn't erase local culture — it blends with it in unique ways.

twist.text: Local cultures aren't just victims. They actively reshape global influences to fit their own needs and values.

climax.text: Glocalization shows that culture flows in multiple directions. The global and local constantly transform each other.

punchline.text: Global meets local and both change.

quiz.question: What does glocalization mean?
options: Global products being adapted to fit local cultures
